mechanism_narrative: Positive real activity momentum, measured by rising GDPC1, raises expected corporate revenues and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when the expansion signal persists.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 13260.5 as of the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth signals are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of triggered walk-forward observations or the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and recession risk, causing investors to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 9.0% or rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 9.1 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, real purchasing power falls and policy lacks room to offset the shock, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 226.268 and FEDFUNDS was 0.1
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-low and CPI-rising condition has a nonnegative 20-day average excess return versus baseline or if CPIAUCSL adds no incremental predictive power after conditioning on FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A break lower in housing starts signals weak household formation, tight mortgage credit, and reduced construction-linked demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 650 thousand units or falls more than 10% year over year
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 604.0 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-threshold episodes are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 45% of cases or the triggered mean return is statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An upside crossing in industrial production indicates firms are increasing output and inventory restocking is active, which raises near-term cyclical earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 94.0419 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO accelerations have a 5-day hit rate at or below 50% for positive S&P 500 returns or a rolling 2-year Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accompanied by falling unemployment identifies a self-reinforcing consumer demand cycle, raising expected revenues for index constituents and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 389502.0 and UNRATE was 9.1
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either single-series condition over 20 trading days or if triggered excess returns are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently near-zero federal funds rate lowers discount rates and increases the relative attractiveness of equities versus cash, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS stays at or below 0.25% for at least 6 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.1 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if near-zero FEDFUNDS episodes have lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-zero-rate episodes or if the positive return hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains that fail to reduce unemployment signal low-quality labor recovery and weak income transmission, causing equity investors to discount future demand and producing negative short-horizon returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months while UNRATE does not fall over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 131132.0 and UNRATE was 9.1
failure_condition: Reject if the payroll-up but unemployment-not-down condition is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if interaction terms are insignificant in walk-forward tests.
